title: Issues 2060 2062 2064 lane remainder

execplan_profile.projection_rule: canonical_core is authoritative for intent, scope, next action, proof, continuation, and closeout; legacy fields remain compatibility projections.

canonical_core.requested_outcome: Implement the remaining payload/dogfooding follow-ups from the just-merged #2058/#2059 lane as a stacked PR sequence: #2060 issue evidence reuse, #2064 bounded PR-comment repair closeout, and #2062 current-state payload upgrade attention planning.
canonical_core.hard_constraints: Do not weaken planning closeout for broad lane completion, do not treat chat summaries as durable issue evidence, do not make start mutate the repo, do not overwrite host-owned payload surfaces, and keep long-hop upgrade work structured/recheckable rather than prose-only.
canonical_core.agent_may_decide: Exact stack boundaries, helper names, packet shapes, and focused test placement while preserving each issue's acceptance boundary.
canonical_core.escalate_when: A better-looking fix changes the requested outcome, owned surface, time horizon, or meaningful validation story.
canonical_core.next_action: Implement #2060 first, then #2064, then #2062 as stacked branches from current master.
proof_expectations: Focused CLI/runtime tests for each issue slice.
AW implement proof selection after changed paths are known.
Schema/reference/generated command checks when public packets or command surfaces change.
Workspace lint/typecheck and relevant broad workspace tests before PR publication.
completion_criteria: #2060 exposes a narrow structured issue-evidence refresh/import path so already-fetched issue data can satisfy external-intent grounding without trusting chat summaries.
#2064 lets bounded PR-comment repair work claim only PR-feedback-addressed progress without requiring active-plan closeout residue, while preserving broad lane/issue completion blockers.
#2062 adds a structured current-state payload upgrade attention plan for long-hop payload upgrades, with categories, affected surfaces, recheck command, and startup/doctor/report visibility.
The stack is published as PRs with semver labels and ready state after local and GitHub proof pass.

goal: Create a bounded plan for Issues 2060 2062 2064 lane remainder.
non_goals: Leave adjacent backlog or follow-on work out of this plan.

intent.escalation: Escalate when a better-looking fix changes the requested outcome, owned surface, time horizon, or meaningful validation story.

execution_bounds.allowed paths: Workspace runtime/startup/implement/report helpers, config/schema/reference docs, generated command packages if command/public schema shape changes, focused CLI tests, and this planning record.
execution_bounds.max changed files: Expect 20-35 files across the full stack if schema/reference/generated surfaces change.
execution_bounds.required validation commands: Focused pytest per slice; AW implement --changed per slice; make test-workspace; make lint-workspace; make typecheck; schema/reference/generated checks for public contract changes; runtime mirror consistency; git diff --check.
execution_bounds.ask-before-refactor threshold: Ask before broadening beyond the promoted item.
execution_bounds.stop before touching: Unrelated backlog, adjacent modules, or canonical contracts not named by this plan.

stop_conditions.stop when: The work no longer matches the promoted item or its completion criteria.
stop_conditions.escalate when boundary reached: A correct fix requires changing the requested outcome or ownership boundary.
stop_conditions.escalate on scope drift: Implementation needs files outside the filled execution bounds.
stop_conditions.escalate on proof failure: The selected proof cannot demonstrate the completion criteria.

context_budget.pre-work config pull: Use compact config/startup/summary outputs before opening raw planning or routing files.
context_budget.pre-work memory pull: Route to the narrowest relevant memory only when the task needs durable repo knowledge.
context_budget.tiny resumability note: Archived slice is complete; reopen only if closeout evidence is invalidated.
context_budget.context-shift triggers: Proof failure, scope drift, interruption, handoff, or closeout.
